features.summary: Costco reported strong Q1 2021 results with net income of $1.166 billion ($2.62 per share) versus $844 million ($1.90 per share) last year. Net sales increased 16.9% to $42.35 billion, with comparable sales up 15.4% (17.1% excluding gas deflation and FX). E-commerce sales surged 86.2% year-over-year, representing about 7% of total sales. The company opened 8 net new warehouses during the quarter and maintained strong membership metrics with 59.1 million paid households. COVID-related premium wages cost $212 million pre-tax, extended through January 3rd.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued strong performance driven by sustained demand for home-related products as consumers redirect spending from travel and dining. The company plans to open 20-22 new warehouses for the full year with capital expenditures of $3.0-3.2 billion. Supply chain challenges including container shortages and port congestion are expected to persist until March 2021. Management remains cautious about inventory planning given uncertain demand patterns but feels confident in the company's ability to serve members effectively through the pandemic recovery.
features.risks: Key risks include potential normalization of sales patterns as COVID restrictions ease and consumers return to pre-pandemic spending habits on travel and dining. Supply chain disruptions continue with container shortages, port congestion, and delivery delays affecting inventory availability. The company faces margin pressure from COVID-related premium wages ($212 million in Q1) and investment in Costco Logistics (6 basis point margin headwind). Renewal rates showed slight decline in Canada due to pandemic-related deferrals, and there's uncertainty around the sustainability of current elevated sales levels.
features.key_drivers: Strong sales growth was driven by increased shopping frequency (up 5.5% worldwide) and higher average transaction sizes (up 9.4%). Fresh foods led margin improvements through reduced spoilage and improved labor productivity, while reduced promotional activity across the industry supported pricing. E-commerce acceleration and successful integration of big-and-bulky delivery through Costco Logistics contributed to growth. The shift in consumer spending from services to goods, particularly home-related categories like electronics, furniture, and exercise equipment, provided significant tailwinds to comparable sales performance.
